summary: No ship: the RED record matches red.sha and fails for L9 as claimed, but the plan introduces an uncharacterized fail-open for supported RollingUpdate deployments. Simpler alternative: retain the existing `image_id` repository selector as a materialization guard and change only the `label_replace` sources to `image_spec`.

findings.title: Blocker: the spec-only selector lets a non-running replacement suppress real drift
findings.body: Line 110 replaces the `image_id` selector with `image_spec`. The chart explicitly supports RollingUpdate, where an old ready Pod can remain while its replacement cannot pull the new image. KSM constructs `kube_pod_container_info` by pairing the spec image with status `imageID` without requiring a running container ([official source](https://github.com/kubernetes/kube-state-metrics/blob/main/internal/store/pod.go)); Kubernetes also classifies time spent pulling an image as Pending ([API reference](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.29/)). The replacement can therefore advertise `image_spec=NEW` with an empty `image_id`; the planned RHS matches NEW and suppresses the alert although only OLD is running. The current selector excludes that replacement and continues detecting drift. L1 and L9 use single-Pod scenarios, so this second behavior flip is unprotected.
findings.recommendation: Add a preserved-contract leg containing an OLD running Pod plus a NEW pull-failed Pod with empty `image_id`, and require continued firing. Keep the twin `image_id=~"ghcr…"` selectors while extracting app/digest from `image_spec`, or explicitly join against running-container status.

next_steps: Revise B-1 and the characterization suite to cover the failed RollingUpdate ordering before implementation.
Open question: should `ImageDigestDrift` remain a running-image alert or become desired-spec-only? The latter is a second behavior change and should move to gated-pipeline.
